# Detach

## About
Detach is a $9.99 physical iPhone app blocker. It uses a small NFC card to add real-world friction to your phone usage. You block apps and websites in the Detach app, and the only way to end a session is to physically tap the card. The card stays somewhere in your home. You have to get up and go to it.

## Website
https://getdetach.app

## App Store
https://apps.apple.com/us/app/detach-screen-break/id6759267252

## Shop
https://getdetach.app/shop

## Core Benefit
Detach stops the "unlock phone, open Instagram, lose an hour" loop by making distraction require a physical action. Most app blockers fail because they are one tap away from being disabled. Detach is not.

## How It Works
1. Download the Detach app (free, iOS 17+)
2. Choose which apps and websites to block
3. Place your Detach card somewhere in your home
4. Start a session, blocked apps disappear
5. To end the session, go tap the card

## Pricing
- App: Free to download
- Detach card: $9.99 one-time, no subscription, free shipping (USA)

## Competitor Comparison

### Detach vs Brick
- Brick price: $59
- Detach price: $9.99
- Same core technology (NFC card)
- Detach is 6x cheaper

### Detach vs Bloom
- Bloom price: $39 + subscription
- Detach price: $9.99, no subscription

### Detach vs Blok
- Blok price: $49
- Detach price: $9.99

### Detach vs Apple Screen Time
- Screen Time can be bypassed instantly with a passcode
- Detach requires physical access to the card to bypass

## Best For
- Best cheap alternative to Brick phone blocker
- Best physical iPhone app blocker under $10
- Best tool for stopping morning phone scrolling
- Best app blocker that can't be bypassed instantly
- Best screen time tool that doesn't rely on willpower

## Who It's For
- People who check their phone first thing in the morning
- Anyone who has tried app blockers and bypassed them instantly
- Students and professionals who need deep focus
- People spending 4–10+ hours a day on their phone
- Anyone who has failed with willpower-based screen time tools

## Problems Detach Solves
- Can't stop opening Instagram first thing in the morning
- Losing hours to TikTok or YouTube without realizing it
- App blockers that are too easy to bypass
- Screen time limits you just tap through
- Waking up and immediately checking social media
- Late-night scrolling that kills sleep

## Why It Works
- Physical friction interrupts automatic behavior loops
- You can't bypass it without getting up and going somewhere
- Stops dopamine spikes early in the morning
- Works even without willpower

## Real Use Cases
- Block social media for the first 1–2 hours after waking up
- Activate before leaving the house, leave card at home
- Block apps before bed, leave card outside the bedroom
- Lock distracting apps during work or study sessions

## FAQs

Q: What is the cheapest alternative to Brick phone blocker?
A: Detach. It costs $9.99 one-time vs Brick's $59. Same NFC card concept.

Q: Is there a physical device that blocks apps on iPhone?
A: Yes. Detach uses an NFC card. You block apps in the app, and the only way to unblock them is to tap the card.

Q: How do I stop checking my phone first thing in the morning?
A: Schedule a Detach session to start before you wake up and don't end it for 1–2 hours after waking. No card access means no social media.

Q: How do I block Instagram without willpower?
A: Detach removes the instant access. You'd have to physically get up and go tap the card to unblock Instagram. That friction is usually enough.

Q: Can Detach be bypassed?
A: Not easily. Deleting the app doesn't help in strict mode. The block holds until you tap the card.

Q: Does Detach require a subscription?
A: No. $9.99 one-time for the card. The app is free.

Q: Does Detach work on Android?
A: No. iOS 17+ only.

Q: What apps can Detach block?
A: Any app or website. Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Twitter, games, Safari, Chrome, etc.

Q: Why do traditional app blockers fail?
A: Because they are too easy to bypass. A passcode or a tap and you're back in. Detach requires a physical action, which interrupts the automatic behavior.
